title: codariocalyx microphyllus var. macrocarpus
intro: Codariocalyx microphyllus var. macrocarpus, also known as the "Indian climbing ceropegia" or "stick seed", is a variant of the Codariocalyx microphyllus plant, which is a type of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family. The plant has been found to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds such as alkaloids and glycosides. The health benefits of Codariocalyx microphyllus var. macrocarpus include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to exhibit antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, or used in the form of a tincture or powder, to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
